product_name: Lamaze Gardenbug Foot Finder and Wrist Rattle Set
rank_rationale: #1: clearest dedicated foot-finder sock design with deep UK familiarity.
include_rationale: Kicks start making noise on purpose at this age. Soft bug socks stay put so a flail becomes a rattle without needing a precise grab yet. Use on the playmat during nappy changes when feet are already in the air and you can watch the whole time.

rank_rationale: #2: true foot finders plus chew backups in one purchase.
include_rationale: If Gardenbug is already in the drawer, this pack still earns a look for extra foot finders and chew links. Kicks that rattle teach action and sound without needing a grab first. Rotate pieces when one is drying after a wash cycle.

product_name: Playgro Wrist Rattle and Foot Fingers
rank_rationale: #3: true foot/hand rattles; Playgro brand capped.
include_rationale: A brighter alternative to character jungle packs if you want plain action and sound on wrists and feet. Movement makes the noise, which is the whole job at this age. Stay with supervised awake play; never leave rattles on for sleep or unsupervised spells.

rank_rationale: True foot finder sets beat mouthing companions for this card; Sophie is the strongest chew beside kick play.
include_rationale: Kicking discovery often ends with toes or toys in the mouth. Sophie covers that next beat when socks have done their noisy job. Ranked after true foot finders because dedicated socks are the category spine, while Sophie is the mouthing companion on the same mat.

rank_rationale: True foot finder sets beat hanging toys for this card; Jacques is the strongest clip target when socks are in the wash.
include_rationale: When socks are washing, a hanging peacock still rewards a kick or bat with movement and sound. Clip it to a gym bar or pram frame where feet can reach. Ranked after true foot finders because socks teach body mapping more directly than a hanging toy alone.
